CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court 3.1385. Judge: James Robertson; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Continued To Feb-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Moot
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVEREST MWAMBA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot, in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FRANK BROWN'S Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall file his proposed Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") within 10 days of entry of this Order. The case is at its early stages (no answer has been filed, discovery has yet to commence, and no trial date has been set) and Defendants have not established they will be unduly prejudiced by the filing of the amended complaint. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428.) Plaintiff's request to have the proposed SAC deemed filed and served is denied. Plaintiff must file and serve the SAC pursuant to statute and local rules. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EVEREST MWAMBA'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. It is unclear how the filing of the petition of separation transferred Defendant Maureen Barbara Kenney's ("Kenney") community property interest in the property listed in paragraph 8 of the complaint. The petition of separation, attached as Exhibit A to the complaint, states at paragraph 4 that it is "to be determined" what assets and debts are to be confirmed as separate property. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts illustrating how the filing of the petition of separation transferred Kenney's community property rights. Additionally, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to comply with the requirements of Cal. Rule of Court 2.112. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/EHG
